task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the criteria.
trace: The approach here is to first ensure that the list has at least two distinct values by checking its length after converting it into a set and back to a list. If the condition isn't met, we return `None`. Otherwise, we sort the list in descending order and return the second element (the second largest value). The key advantage of this approach is that it handles the edge case where there are fewer than two distinct values, as well as providing concise and readable code.
